figures.caption: FIG. 5. Néel vector switching in an antiferromagnet. (a) Schematic of an antiferromagnetic memory device with orthogonal writing terminals for current injection and diagonal probes for readout via anisotropic Hall magnetoresistance. The blue and red double arrows denote two orthogonal orientations of the Néel vector, corresponding to the binary memory states (0 and 1). (b) Time evolution of the normalized Néel vector components under Néel spin–orbit torque (NSOT) driven by asymmetric impurity scattering. The switching between the two states occurs on a timescale of ∼ 5 ps. (c) Three-dimensional switching trajectory of the Néel vector during the reversal process. m̂A and m̂B denote unit vectors along the magnetization of the two opposite sublattices.
